headline.task: forecast forward 5-day realized vol from past 20-day realized vol function

key_findings: The cascade slope is far from optimal for forecasting forward vol. Its test-set Spearman is 0.09, much lower than the headline -0.20 on the full sample.
FNO achieves Spearman 0.59 on the test set, 6.6x better than the cascade slope.
DeepONet achieves Spearman 0.62 on the test set, 6.9x better than the cascade slope.
The hand-crafted cascade operator captures only a fraction of the signal in the realized vol function. The learned operator (DeepONet) extracts the rest.
The cascade remains valuable for interpretability and as a strong pre-registered baseline. The operator learning is a separate, complementary direction.
